title: React pricing page — region-aware plans, Razorpay Checkout, post-payment polling
acceptance_criteria: Pricing.tsx: on mount, calls GET /api/payment/plans; renders a loading state while fetching; renders a 'Contact admin' state if 503 (payment not configured).

Pricing.tsx: when plans arrive and isIndia=true or confidence=unknown — renders both monthly_pass and credits_pack plan cards with INR prices clearly labelled; UPI icon shown; credits_pack card shows '{{N}} feature credits' description.
Pricing.tsx: when isIndia=false and confidence=high — renders a message 'India UPI payments only in this release. International payments coming soon.' No plan cards shown.

Pricing.tsx: on plan card click — calls POST /api/payment/create-order with the planId; on success, opens Razorpay Checkout widget with the returned orderId, amountPaise, currency, razorpayKeyId. The Razorpay script tag is loaded lazily (not in index.html) to avoid loading it on every page.
usePayment hook: encapsulates the create-order call and Checkout widget lifecycle. Exposes { initPayment(planId), isLoading, error }.

After Razorpay Checkout reports success (handler.payment_id present): enters polling loop calling GET /api/payment/access-status. Uses exponential backoff starting at 1s, max 10 retries. On hasAccess=true: calls onSuccess() callback which navigates to dashboard. On exhausting retries: shows inline message 'Your payment was received. Access is being activated — please refresh in a moment.' — no error state, no redirect.

useAccessStatus hook: calls GET /api/payment/access-status on mount; exposes { hasAccess, expiresAt, daysRemaining, isLoading }. Used by the dashboard layout to show a 'X days remaining' badge and by App.tsx to gate the dashboard route.
App.tsx: modified ONLY to add a /pricing route (public) and to wrap the dashboard route with an access check using useAccessStatus (redirect to /pricing if !hasAccess and !isLoading).
No hardcoded prices in frontend — all amounts come from the /api/payment/plans response.
RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET is never referenced in any frontend file — only RAZORPAY_KEY_ID (public key) is used via the orderId response.
output_format: all three new files plus the App.tsx diff, plus plain-English note on why Razorpay script is loaded lazily and why the polling uses 10 retries with backoff rather than a hard timeout
